name: Hinterview Architecture

productIntent.summary: Desktop-first and web-capable HLD and LLD interview simulator with AI evaluation, diagramming, secure key storage, local speech-to-text, and structured question journeys.
productIntent.keyDecision: Use a shared web application with a desktop shell instead of trying to make Express.js itself the desktop runtime.

frontend.ui: Tailwind CSS plus custom component primitives
frontend.validation: Zod shared across client and server
frontend.editor: Plain text-first answer editor with optional richer formatting later
frontend.diagramming: Excalidraw integration or a custom canvas wrapper around a scene model

backend.jobs: In-process queue for v1, upgradeable to background workers later

desktop.ipcBoundary: Minimal desktop-only APIs exposed via preload

storage.desktop: SQLite for app data plus OS keychain or encrypted local secret storage
storage.web: PostgreSQL for user data plus server-side encrypted secret storage
storage.blob: Local filesystem on desktop, object storage on web for audio and exports

goals: Support built-in and custom system design questions
Force ordered completion of interview stages while still showing the full journey
Collect user answer text, transcripts, diagram scene data, scores, and learnings
Support HLD and LLD practice modes under one shared problem catalog
Support multiple AI providers and models selected per session
Ship one shared product across desktop and web
assumptions: The app starts as a single-user product without mandatory auth in desktop mode.
Web mode may later require auth and hosted persistence.
Interview readiness is the scoring target, not production infrastructure accuracy.
Grammar, spelling, and shorthand endpoint notation should not materially affect scoring.
Questions must stay constrained to the scoped problem area and avoid drifting into unrelated subdomains.
Hint, answer, and scoring can all be provider-agnostic through one adapter layer.

featureModules.name: AI Agent Settings
responsibilities: Store provider prompts and capability tags

featureModules.name: Custom Question Generator
responsibilities: Generate HLD and LLD variants when applicable without duplicating the base problem record
Generate hints, answer expectations, and rubric

featureModules.name: Interview Session Engine
responsibilities: Track selected mode such as HLD or LLD
Persist draft answers and diagram state
Trigger hint, answer, and scoring actions

featureModules.name: Diagram Canvas
responsibilities: Capture HLD scene data and optional LLD diagrams
Export image and normalized diagram summary for AI

featureModules.name: AI Evaluation Service
responsibilities: Keep prompts constrained to the declared problem focus
Score response and return coaching feedback

primaryFlows.name: Start Built-In Interview
steps: User opens question library
User selects question
User selects HLD or LLD mode when available
User selects AI agent/model
System creates interview session
Stage 1 unlocks and later stages remain read-only
User answers by text or audio and updates diagram
System saves progress continuously
User requests hint or answer if needed
User submits stage for evaluation
System decrements tries if score is 8 or below
System reveals the reference answer after max tries are exhausted
System unlocks next stage only after solve or forced reveal flow

primaryFlows.name: Create Custom Question
steps: User submits a raw system design prompt
AI generator builds stages, rubric, hints, answer outlines, difficulty, and focus areas
User reviews generated structure
System saves it into the question library
User starts a normal interview flow

primaryFlows.name: Review Learning
steps: System aggregates prior scoring data
AI produces generalized system design learning themes
User reviews themes in My Learning
User edits and adds personal learning notes
User revisits relevant prior attempts

relationships: Question has many QuestionStage across HLD and LLD modes without duplicating the base problem
InterviewSession belongs to Question and AgentProfile
LearningInsight aggregates across many StageAttempt records

restEndpoints.purpose: List built-in and custom questions with progress summary, difficulty, focus area, and mode availability
Generate and persist a structured custom question
Fetch question metadata and ordered stages for HLD or LLD mode
Create an interview session for a question, selected mode, and selected agent
Fetch session progress and stage states
Autosave draft answer and diagram state
Generate a hint for the current stage
Return the reference answer for the current stage
Score the current stage, update tries, and unlock the next one if solved or completed through reveal flow
Convert recorded audio into transcript text locally on device
Fetch generalized and user-authored learning insights
Edit a user-authored or user-curated learning note

providerAbstraction.selectionModel: Each session binds to one active agent profile, but settings may define many.

inputs: Diagram scene summary and optional export image
principles: Reward clarity, prioritization, and interview communication
Do not penalize grammar, spelling, or shorthand notation when intent is clear
Avoid penalizing lack of production-detail depth unless it blocks correctness
Keep evaluation constrained to the question scope and declared focus area

requirements: Never store plaintext API keys in app logs
Desktop mode should prefer OS keychain access
If keychain is unavailable, use envelope encryption with a machine/user bound secret
Web mode must keep secrets server-side only
Prompt payloads should avoid sending unnecessary personal metadata
Raw audio must never be sent to AI providers

desktopApproach.preferred: Keytar or native keychain binding for provider API keys
desktopApproach.fallback: AES-GCM encrypted value stored in SQLite with key material derived from a local secure secret plus app salt

webApproach.preferred: KMS-backed encryption for stored secrets on the server
webApproach.browserRule: Do not persist raw provider keys in browser localStorage or IndexedDB

localAiAdjacencies: On-device speech-to-text runtime or model binding

stateManagement.schemaLayer: Zod schemas shared between renderer, server, and Electron boundaries where possible

autosavePolicy.answerDraft: debounced every few seconds and on blur

covers: Repo setup with React + TypeScript + Vite renderer
HLD and LLD mode selection on shared problems
Problem-focused stage generation instead of boilerplate templates

openQuestions: Should web mode support bring-your-own-key or only platform-managed keys?
Should scoring happen after every stage or optionally after the full question as well?
How much Excalidraw parity is necessary for v1 versus embedding the library directly?
Should hints consume a limited budget per question?
Should custom question generation be editable before first use?
Which local speech-to-text dependency gives the best balance of macOS packaging size and acceptable latency?
